patterns_matched: This repository uses **Twilio Voice SDK (`Twilio.Device`)** for browser-based WebRTC calling. Telnyx WebRTC is **fundamentally different** in architecture:
| WebRTC (Twilio.Device) | ⚠️ **ARCHITECTURAL CHANGE** | Full rewrite to TelnyxRTC
| WebRTC (Twilio.Device) | @telnyx/webrtc SDK | public/js/browser-calls.js | High | Full client SDK migration required
|⚠️ **CRITICAL: This repository uses Twilio.Client (Twilio Voice WebRTC SDK)**
The Twilio Voice SDK (`Twilio.Device`) is a **browser-based WebRTC client**. Telnyx has a different approach:
| **WebRTC client SDK is fundamentally different** | Certain | High | Twilio.Device vs TelnyxRTC - complete API rewrite required
Twilio.Client Access Tokens incompatible | Certain | High | Must replace with SIP credentials
No direct Telnyx equivalent for Twilio.Device features | Medium | Medium | Some event handling patterns differ |

config_files.line: - Some residual references in scan files (twilio-scan.json, twilio-deep-scan.json) - these are expected as they are generated scan artifacts, not source code
- Migration branch: `migrate/twilio-to-telnyx` (to be created in Phase 3)
> Check out the [JavaScript SDK Quickstarts](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/sdks/javascript/get-started) to get started with browser-based calling.

Learn how to use [Twilio's JavaScript SDK](https://www.twilio.com/docs/voice/sdks/javascript) to make browser-to-phone and browser-to-browser calls with ease. The unsatisfied customers of the Birchwood Bicycle Polo Co. need your help!
| TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID  | Your primary Twilio account identifier - find this [in the Console](https://www.twilio.com/console).|
| TWILIO_NUMBER | A Twilio phone number in [E.164 format](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/E.164) - you can [get one here](https://www.twilio.com/console/phone-numbers/incoming) |

| TWILIO_APP_SID | The TwiML application with a voice URL configured to access your server running this app - create one [in the console here](https://www.twilio.com/console/voice/twiml/apps) and use its `Sid`. Also, you will need to configure the Voice "REQUEST URL" on the TwiML app once you've got your server up and running. |

| TWILIO_API_KEY / TWILIO_API_SECRET | Your REST API Key information needed to create an [Access Token](https://www.twilio.com/docs/iam/access-tokens) - create [one here](https://www.twilio.com/console/project/api-keys). |
[Create a new TwiML app](https://www.twilio.com/console/voice/twiml/apps) and use its `Sid` as the `TWILIO_APP_SID` environment variable wherever you run this app.

Once you have created your TwiML app, [configure your Twilio phone number](https://support.twilio.com/hc/en-us/articles/223180928-How-Do-I-Create-a-TwiML-App-). If you don't have a Twilio phone number yet, you can purchase a new number in your [Twilio Account Dashboard](https://www.twilio.com/console/phone-numbers/search).
See [Twilio Account Settings](#twilio-account-settings) to locate the necessary environment variables.
localhost. [Here](https://www.twilio.com/blog/2015/09/6-awesome-reasons-to-use-ngrok-when-testing-webhooks.html), you could read more about why we recommend you to use ngrok.
- The CodeExchange repository can be found [here](https://github.com/twilio-labs/code-exchange/).
reported by contacting the project team at open-source@twilio.com. All

| TWILIO_API_KEY / TWILIO_API_SECRET | Your REST API Key information needed to create an [Access Token](https://www.twilio.com/docs/iam/access-tokens) - create [one here](https://www.twilio.com/console/project/api-keys). |
[Create a new TwiML app](https://www.twilio.com/console/voice/twiml/apps) and use its `Sid` as the `TWILIO_APP_SID` environment variable wherever you run this app.

Once you have created your TwiML app, [configure your Twilio phone number](https://support.twilio.com/hc/en-us/articles/223180928-How-Do-I-Create-a-TwiML-App-). If you don't have a Twilio phone number yet, you can purchase a new number in your [Twilio Account Dashboard](https://www.twilio.com/console/phone-numbers/search).
See [Twilio Account Settings](#twilio-account-settings) to locate the necessary environment variables.

[Create a new TwiML app](https://www.twilio.com/console/voice/twiml/apps) and use its `Sid` as the `TWILIO_APP_SID` environment variable wherever you run this app.

Once you have created your TwiML app, [configure your Twilio phone number](https://support.twilio.com/hc/en-us/articles/223180928-How-Do-I-Create-a-TwiML-App-). If you don't have a Twilio phone number yet, you can purchase a new number in your [Twilio Account Dashboard](https://www.twilio.com/console/phone-numbers/search).
See [Twilio Account Settings](#twilio-account-settings) to locate the necessary environment variables.

reported by contacting the project team at open-source@twilio.com. All
This repository uses **Twilio Voice SDK (`Twilio.Device`)** for browser-based WebRTC calling. Telnyx WebRTC is **fundamentally different** in architecture:
1. **Replaced** Twilio Access Token generation with Telnyx SIP credential generation
4. **Updated** all environment variables from Twilio to Telnyx equivalents
| WebRTC (Twilio.Device) | ⚠️ **ARCHITECTURAL CHANGE** | Full rewrite to TelnyxRTC |
- Updated script src from Twilio SDK to Telnyx WebRTC CDN

This migration successfully transitioned a Twilio Voice WebRTC application to Telnyx WebRTC. The **most significant change** was the architectural shift from Twilio's mandatory backend token server to Telnyx's optional direct-SIP approach.
> Generated by the Telnyx Twilio Migration Skill
| Twilio Products in Use | Voice, WebRTC (Browser Client) |
| Twilio Product | Telnyx Replacement | Files Affected | Complexity | Notes |
| WebRTC (Twilio.Device) | @telnyx/webrtc SDK | public/js/browser-calls.js | High | Full client SDK migration required |
⚠️ **CRITICAL: This repository uses Twilio.Client (Twilio Voice WebRTC SDK)**
The Twilio Voice SDK (`Twilio.Device`) is a **browser-based WebRTC client**. Telnyx has a different approach:
- Twilio requires a backend token server (routes/token.js generates AccessTokens)
| **WebRTC client SDK is fundamentally different** | Certain | High | Twilio.Device vs TelnyxRTC - complete API rewrite required |
| Twilio.Client Access Tokens incompatible | Certain | High | Must replace with SIP credentials |
| No direct Telnyx equivalent for Twilio.Device features | Medium | Medium | Some event handling patterns differ |

This project is configured to use a **TwiML App**, which allows us to easily set the Voice URLs for all Twilio phone numbers we purchase in this app.

Once you have created your TwiML app, [configure your Twilio phone number](https://support.twilio.com/hc/en-us/articles/223180928-How-Do-I-Create-a-TwiML-App-). If you don't have a Twilio phone number yet, you can purchase a new number in your [Twilio Account Dashboard](https://www.twilio.com/console/phone-numbers/search).
See [Twilio Account Settings](#twilio-account-settings) to locate the necessary environment variables.

4. Follow the steps in [Local Development](#local-development) on how to expose your port to Twilio using a tool like [ngrok](https://ngrok.com/) and configure the remaining parts of your application.
* Migrated from Twilio Client to Telnyx WebRTC SDK

notes: Standard TWILIO_* env vars are scanned above. Also search for non-standard names that may contain Twilio SIDs or phone numbers (e.g., ACCOUNT_SID, SMS_FROM, PHONE_NUMBER).
